scenario: Jun 14: US-Iran MOU text finalised (Pakistan PM Sharif); Trump announces deal; US naval blockade lifted; Hormuz to reopen after signing Jun 19 Switzerland as 'permanently toll free.' Brent $79.45 (TradingEconomics Jun 17, -19% from Jun 3 $98.57 peak). Physical reopening constrained: mine-clearing ~6 months from signing; Ras Laffan LNG restart not before late Aug 2026. Jun 5 US-Iran drone/radar exchange; Jun 9-10 Apache shootdown + US 49 Tomahawk retaliation near Tehran preceded deal push. Tail 15% (deal collapse). Commodity markets pricing supply resumption — but supply lag in LNG and fertilizers limits reversal pace.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels. Deal announcement reduces near-term cost pressure but mine-clearing and Qafco restart are the binding constraints on supply normalisation.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat normalising on deal announcement (Jun 14 MOU; Brent $79.45 Jun 17, -19% from Jun 3 peak). Freight cost premium unwinding as Cape rerouting eases with Hormuz reopening expected Jun 19. Qafco force majeure lifting timeline still uncertain — mine-clearing limits full normalisation. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Corn normalising on crude correlation (Brent $79.45 Jun 17, -19% from peak). Biofuel demand outlook softer with oil lower. Cape routing cost premium unwinding on deal. Tail 15%. Source: CME ZCK26 (est.).

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Soybeans normalising on crude correlation (Brent $79.45 Jun 17). Bunker cost premium easing on Hormuz deal. Cape routing costs unwinding. Tail 15%. Source: CME ZSK26 (est.).

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO easing on crude correlation (Brent $79.45 Jun 17, -19% from peak). Indonesia B40 mandate structural floor intact. Malaysia supply route via Hormuz reopening expected Jun 19. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: Rice normalising as deal reduces food security risk premium (Brent $79.45 Jun 17; tail 15%, down from 42% D93). Qafco force majeure + planting window pressure easing with supply resumption expected. Source: Bangkok export quotes (est.).

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption. Deal announcement reduces near-term pressure, but physical flows through Hormuz depend on mine-clearing timeline (~6 months from signing).

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha normalising on crude (Brent $79.45 Jun 17, -19% from peak). Gulf condensate supply resumption expected post-mine clearance. Mine-clearing ~6 months from Jun 19 signing. Tail 15%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene easing on naphtha/crude normalisation (Jun 17). SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks persist until mine-clearing (~6 months). Tail 15%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol below 90d baseline on deal news (Brent $79.45 Jun 17). Qatar force majeure lifting timeline tethered to mine-clearing and Ras Laffan restart. Sanctions waivers under deal terms may ease some OFAC constraints. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Deal announcement reduces long-run supply risk, but Hormuz mine-clearing (~6 months from Jun 19 signing) is the binding constraint on physical export resumption.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea still elevated despite deal (zscore 2.13). Qafco force majeure lifting tethered to mine-clearing (~6 months from Jun 19); planting window demand acute. Down sharply from D93 $745 peak but supply cannot normalise quickly. Tail 15%: deal collapse spikes back toward $700+. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP easing on deal but still above baseline (zscore 1.20). Saudi Ma'aden shipping resumption pending mine-clearing. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam acute — demand not abating quickly. Source: Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight premium easing on oil normalisation (Brent $79.45 Jun 17). Cape rerouting cost unwinding as Hormuz reopening expected. Palm oil plantations: input cost pressure easing. Source: Argus Fertilizers / CRU (est.).

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Deal announcement Jun 14 triggers Cape premium unwinding: carriers pricing Hormuz re-opening and rerouting reversal. Physical re-entry contingent on mine-clearing (~6 months from Jun 19 signing) and P&I club re-authorisation — full shipping normalisation is a late-2026 event.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI falling sharply as deal removes crisis premium (Brent $79.45, -19% from D93; Hormuz reopening Jun 19). Cape rerouting premium unwinding. Mine-clearing ~6 months constrains full reversal. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates falling on deal announcement (D104 Jun 14). Hormuz reopening expected Jun 19; P&I clubs will re-enter progressively post-mine clearance. AG-Asia rates normalising toward pre-crisis 65-90 WS range. Source: Seatrade Maritime / Clarkson (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI declining as Asia-Europe Cape premium unwinds (deal Jun 14; Hormuz signing Jun 19). War risk surcharges being lifted by carriers. Full normalisation: 2-3 months post-mine clearance. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly, est.).
